user_message: Need help with Wide World Importers -- pull the context. The CSA flagged this as at-risk yesterday.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 09:28 EST
Subject: competitive positioning - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-01-04 10:22 EST
Subject: Re: competitive positioning Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the competitive positioning proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: competitive positioning Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-01-21
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), David (Wide World Importers)

[00:02:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on competitive positioning from our last session.

[00:05:00] David (Wide World Importers): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:09:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:13:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:18:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:21:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:25:00] David (Wide World Importers): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:30:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:33:00] David (Wide World Importers): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Wide World Importers: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Stakeholder Map Generation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
